text: The outside of this exhibit space feels sterile, almost clinical, with the hard lines and cold glass that separate me from what's inside. But as I move closer and enter, I am greeted by words that echo vulnerability and complexity—"exposed," "confused," "misunderstood." I feel strangely seen, as if the room itself acknowledges the awkwardness that sits quietly within me. There's an invitation here: an encouragement to explore, to be open, to notice the difference between intention and perception. I feel contemplative, somewhat vulnerable, but quietly hopeful in this strange, thoughtful space.
